//: // This file tells Vercel how to serve the React frontend in production.
//   Our app is a Single Page Application (SPA). That means there's only ONE
//   HTML file (index.html) and React handles all 'pages' with JavaScript.
//   When someone visits 'https://your-app.vercel.app/', Vercel serves index.html
//   and everything works. But if they refresh on a deep URL or bookmark one,
//   Vercel would look for a file at that path and return a 404 error.
//   The 'rewrites' rule below tells Vercel: 'For ANY URL path, serve index.html'.
//   React's JavaScript then reads the URL and renders the right content.
//   1. Go to vercel.com → sign up (free) → 'Add New Project'
//   3. Set 'Root Directory' to 'frontend' (click Edit next to it)
//   4. Vercel auto-detects Vite → build command: npm run build, output: dist/
